loop-cli-main selects first chain target from initial exit code

loop-cli-main assigns chainGroupId and per-task chainName records

Return chain trace metadata so multi-hop fallback remains auditable

single-hop fallback target would become outcome-aware routing
chainGroupId and chainName connect run records
FallbackRoute can expose ordered hops instead of prose-only reason
source loop advances by pointer and stops only on null or missing task
UI direct-self filter does not provide transitive executor guard

source discovers missing target during chain execution
router already has direct missing-target fail-fast behavior to preserve for chains

summary: Outcome-routed fallback chain contract: model success/failure next targets explicitly before making executor fallback multi-hop.
why: The source chooses next work from exit code, while our router has a single failure fallback target.

summary: Route trace metadata: expose chain group and ordered hops so chained fallback stays auditable.
why: The source groups chained run records; our route result currently exposes only action, target, and prose reason.

summary: Cycle-safe chain preflight: add visited-set and max-hop checks before supporting chained fallback routes.
why: The source has conditional chaining but only weak infinite-chain prevention, so our pure router should add explicit guards rather than copy the runtime loop.

summary: Registry pointer validation: fail fast for every configured chain edge before dispatch.
why: The source silently stops on missing chain targets; our router already fails fast for one missing target and should preserve that behavior for multi-hop chains.
